post.description:
Verifies a client's login request and returns a token if it succeeds.

Args:
    db: Database dependency.
    form_data: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm dependency.

Endpoint for registering a new user. Client with registration request must not be logged in.

Args:
    request: Create user request.
    db: Database dependency.
    current_user: Optional user dependency. Should be None for this function to succeed.

Creates a user with metadata request, provided that the current_user has sufficient permissions.

Args:
    db: Database dependency.
    current_user: Current user dependency. Must not be None (e.g. user must be logged in).
    request: Metadata of user to be added.

get.description:
Return the current logged in user.

Args:
    current_user: Current user dependency.

delete.description: Delete the user currently logged in.

get.description:
Get user by username.

Args:
    user_name: Username of user.
    db: Database dependency.

delete.description: Delete the user with user_id if the current user has sufficient permissions to perform this action. Throw an exception if the user currently logged in has insufficient permissions.

get.description: Endpoint for retrieving source works in bulk, optionally filtered by title substring.

post.description:
Create a new source work.

Raises:
    400: Data in some field is too long.

get.description:
Endpoint for retrieving a source work by id.

Raises:
    404: Source work not found (or insufficient permissions).

patch.description:
Update a source work's metadata.

Raises:
    404: Source work not found.
    401: Insufficient permissions.
    400: Data in some field exceeds maximum length.

get.description:
Endpoint for retrieving novels belonging to a source work.

Raises:
    404: Source work not found (or insufficient permissions).

schema.title: Response Read Novels By Source Work Source Works  Sourceworkid  Novels Get

get.description: Endpoint for retrieving novels in bulk.

post.description:
Add a new novel to the database.

Raises:
    404: Language code or source work not found.
    400: Data in some field is too long.

get.description: Endpoint for retrieving novels that the user has special access to.

schema.title: Response Read Novels Mine Novels Mine Get

get.description:
Endpoint for retrieving a novel by id.

Raises:
    404: Novel not found (or insufficient permissions).

patch.description:
Update the novel with novel_id.

Raises:
    404: Novel not found.
    401: Insufficient permissions (user is not owner/editor).
    400: Data in some field exceeds maximum length.

get.description:
Endpoint for retrieving a novel and its associated users.

Raises:
    404: Novel not found (or insufficient permissions).

Endpoint for retrieving chapters by novel_id.

Raises:
    404: Novel not found (or insufficient permissions).

schema.title: Response Read Chapters By Novel Chapters Get

get.description:
Endpoint for retrieving chapter by id.

Raises:
    404: Chapter not found (or insufficient permissions).

patch.description:
Update chapter metadata.

Raises:
    404: Chapter not found.
    401: Insufficient permissions.
    400: Field in request too long.

delete.description:
Delete a chapter from the database.

Raises:
    404: Chapter not found.
    401: Insufficient permissions.
    500: Delete failed for other reasons.

post.description:
Insert a new chapter into the database. Returns chapter metadata and initial empty content.

Raises:
    404: Novel not found.
    409: Chapter with this chapter number already exists in this novel.
    401: Insufficient permissions.

Publish a chapter (make it public).

Raises:
    404: Chapter not found.
    401: Insufficient permissions.

get.description:
Endpoint for retrieving the most recent content of a chapter.

Raises:
    404: Chapter not found, or chapter has no content.

patch.description:
Apply text operations to the most recent content of a chapter. Uses optimistic concurrency
via chapter_content_id -- if the content has been modified since the client last fetched it,
a 409 is returned.

Raises:
    404: Chapter content not found.
    409: Chapter content is outdated (someone else modified it).
    401: Insufficient permissions to modify this chapter.

401.description: Insufficient permissions to modify this chapter.

409.description: Chapter content is outdated, or the request key already exists.

get.description:
Endpoint for retrieving a specific version of chapter content by its id.

Raises:
    404: Chapter content not found (or insufficient permissions).

Endpoint for retrieving all content version metadata for a chapter.
Returns an empty list if the chapter has no content versions or doesn't exist.

schema.title: Response Read Chapter Content Versions Chapters  Chapterid  Content Versions Get

get.description:
Check whether a chapter_content_id is the latest version for a chapter.

Raises:
    404: Chapter content not found (or insufficient read permissions).
    409: Chapter content is outdated.

post.description:
Create one or more chapters and their initial content versions atomically.

Raises:
    400: The upload request cannot be processed.
    401: Authentication failed or the user cannot add chapters to the novel.
    404: The authenticated user or requested novel was not found.
    409: One or more chapter numbers already exist in the novel.
    422: The upload document failed schema validation.

schema.title: Response Create Chapters By Upload Chapters Upload Post

400.description: The upload request cannot be processed.

401.description: Authentication failed or the user cannot add chapters to this novel.

404.description: The authenticated user or requested novel was not found.

409.description: One or more chapter numbers already exist in this novel.

get.description: Gets all label groups of the current user for a novel.

schema.title: Response Read Label Groups Label Groups Get

post.description:
Creates a new label group.

Raises:
    404: Novel not found.
    400: Label group name is too long.

400.description: Label group name is too long.

404.description: Novel associated with this label group not found.

get.description: Gets all label groups of the current user for a novel, along with their roles.

schema.title: Response Read Label Groups With Role Label Groups With Role Get

get.description:
Gets a label group by id.

Raises:
    404: Label group not found (or insufficient permissions).

patch.description:
Updates a label group (e.g. rename).

Raises:
    404: Label group not found.
    400: Label group name is too long.

get.summary: Read Label Datas By Group Chapters
get.description: Gets all label datas in a label group, optionally filtered by chapter range.

schema.title: Response Read Label Datas By Group Chapters Label Datas Get

get.description:
Gets a label data by id.

Raises:
    404: Label data not found (or insufficient permissions).

patch.description:
Applies a stream of edit operations to labels.

Raises:
    404: Label data or its underlying revision text not found, or target label does not exist.
    409: Word mismatch or label overlap detected.
    400: Operation positions out of bounds or invalid operation.

400.description: Operation positions are out of bounds or the operation is otherwise invalid.

404.description: Label data, chapter content, or target label not found.

409.description: Label stream conflict, such as word mismatch, overlap violation, or request-key conflict.

get.description: Get the specific list of labels inside a label data entry.

schema.title: Response Read Labels By Label Data Label Datas  Labeldataid  Labels Get

get.description: Get the list of contributors for a label group.

schema.title: Response Read Label Contributors Label Groups  Labelgroupid  Contributors Get

post.description:
Creates a label data entry for a revision text in a label group.

Raises:
    404: Label group or revision text not found.
    409: Label data for this revision text already exists in this group.

404.description: Label group or chapter content not found.

409.description: Label data already exists for this chapter content in the label group, or the request key already exists.

post.summary: Create Label Datas By Auto Labels
post.description: Creates label datas and populates labels from autolabel results.

get.description:
List autolabel runs for a novel.

Args:
    novel_id: UUID of novel to query runs for.
    mine: If true, only return runs triggered by the current user.

schema.title: Response Read Auto Label Runs Auto Label Runs Get

get.description:
Get autolabel metadata for all autolabels in a run.

Args:
    run_id: UUID of the run.
    start: Optional inclusive lower bound on chapter number.
    end: Optional exclusive upper bound on chapter number.

schema.title: Response Read Auto Labels By Run Auto Label Runs  Runid  Auto Labels Get

get.description:
Retrieve a single autolabel with its label data.

Args:
    auto_label_id: UUID of the autolabel.

post.description:
Create a new autolabel run and autolabels for matching chapters.

The run is created first, then autolabel entries are inserted for each
matching chapter. Worker tasks are dispatched for each autolabel.

get.description:
Retrieves a language by its code.

Args:
    language_code: The code of the language to retrieve.
    db: Database session.

Retrieves all languages in the database.

Args:
    db: Database session.

schema.title: Response Read All Languages Languages Get

get.description:
Retrieves the schemas for all registered filters, including the instance, context, and options schemas for each filter.

Returns:
    A dictionary mapping filter names to their schema information.

schema.title: Response Read Filter Schemas Filters Schemas Get

post.description:
Flags instances using a specified filter and options.

Args:
    filter_name: The name of the filter to use.
    options: The options for flagging instances, which will be validated against the filter's schema.
    db: Database session for any necessary database access during filtering.
    current_user: The user making the request, which may be relevant for certain filters.

schema.title: Response Read Flagged Instances Filters  Filtername  Flag Instances Post

post.description:
Retrieves contexts for a list of instances using a specified filter and options.

Args:
    filter_name: The name of the filter to use.
    instances: The list of instances to retrieve contexts for. Each instance will be validated against the filter's instance schema.
    options: The options for retrieving contexts, which will be validated against the filter's schema.
    db: Database session for any necessary database access during context retrieval.
    current_user: The user making the request, which may be relevant for certain filters.

schema.title: Response Read Contexts Filters  Filtername  Get Contexts Post

post.description:
Decides whether instances pass a filter in given contexts using specified options.

Args:
    filter_name: The name of the filter to use.
    body: An InstanceContextOptions object containing the list of instance-context pairs and the options for deciding instances. Each instance and context will be validated against the filter's schemas.
    db: Database session for any necessary database access during the decision process.
    current_user: The user making the request, which may be relevant for certain filters.

schema.title: Response Read Decisions Filters  Filtername  Decide Instances Post

post.description:
Applies a filter to a label group for a list of instances using specified options.

Args:
    filter_name: The name of the filter to apply.
    body: An InstanceOptions object containing the list of instances and the options for applying the filter. Each instance will be validated against the filter's instance schema, and the options will be validated against the filter's apply filter options schema.
    db: Database session for any necessary database access during filter application.
    current_user: The user making the request, which may be relevant for certain filters.

Gets all data associated with a chapter required for editing.

Args:
    chapter_id: Chapter id of requested chapter
    eager: List of label group IDs for which to fetch eager label data

Raises:
    404: Chapter not found (or insufficient permissions).
    403: Insufficient permissions to access data for other user.

schema.description: List of label group IDs for which to fetch eager label data
schema.title: Eager

post.description:
Fetch label data and labels for specific label groups on a chapter's most
recent content version. Used by the reload group operation.

Raises:
    404: Chapter not found (or insufficient permissions).
    403: Insufficient permissions to access data for other user.

schema.description: Label group IDs to fetch label data and labels for
schema.title: Labelgroupids
Response Read Edit Chapter Label Data Edit Chapter Data  Chapterid  Label Data Post

AddLabelOp.title: AddLabelOp
AddLabelOp.description:
Pydantic schema for a label add operation. Inherits all attributes from LabelOpBase.

Attributes:
    op: The string literal 'add'.
    dirty: Boolean whether to mark the label as dirty.
    entity_group: String representing what entity group this label belongs to. If none, then set a default value.
    score: Float score between 0.0 and 1.0 representing how likely this label is to be an entity.

AutoLabel.title: AutoLabel
AutoLabel.description:
Pydantic schema for a single auto-labeled data entry with its label data.

Attributes:
    auto_label_id: UUID identifier for this AutoLabel.
    auto_label_data: List of labels produced by the NER model, or None if not yet complete.
    auto_label_status: Labeling progress for this autolabel.
    auto_label_message: Details on status (e.g. failure reason).
    auto_label_last_job_id: Job id of last request to autogenerate.
    chapter_content_id: UUID of chapter content this AutoLabel is for.
    run_id: UUID of the AutoLabelRun this autolabel belongs to.

AutoLabelMeta.title: AutoLabelMeta
AutoLabelMeta.description:
Pydantic schema for auto-label metadata (no label data payload).

Attributes:
    auto_label_id: UUID identifier for this AutoLabel.
    auto_label_status: Labeling progress for this autolabel.
    auto_label_message: Details on status.
    auto_label_last_job_id: Job id of last request to autogenerate.
    chapter_content_id: UUID of chapter content this AutoLabel is for.
    run_id: UUID of the AutoLabelRun this autolabel belongs to.

AutoLabelProgress.title: AutoLabelProgress
AutoLabelProgress.description: Status for an autolabel in database. One of 'failed', 'pending', 'processing', 'done'

AutoLabelRun-Input.title: AutoLabelRun
AutoLabelRun-Input.description:
Pydantic schema for an autolabel run (batch).

A run groups autolabels created together in a single request.

Attributes:
    run_id: UUID identifier for this run.
    triggered_by: UUID of user who triggered the run, or None.
    model_name: Name of the NER model used.
    model_params: Parameters for the NER model.
    created_at: When the run was created.

AutoLabelRun-Output.title: AutoLabelRun
AutoLabelRun-Output.description:
Pydantic schema for an autolabel run (batch).

A run groups autolabels created together in a single request.

Attributes:
    run_id: UUID identifier for this run.
    triggered_by: UUID of user who triggered the run, or None.
    model_name: Name of the NER model used.
    model_params: Parameters for the NER model.
    created_at: When the run was created.

Chapter.title: Chapter
Chapter.description:
Pydantic schema for chapter metadata. Represents a single "chapter" entry, which groups all its revisions.

Attributes:
    chapter_id: UUID primary key identifier.
    chapter_num: The chapter number.
    novel_id: UUID foreign key to the novel this chapter belongs to.

ChapterContent.title: ChapterContent
ChapterContent.description:
Pydantic schema for the text content of a chapter.

Attributes:
    chapter_content_text: The full text content of the chapter.
    chapter_content_version: The version number of the text content, used for optimistic concurrency control when updating text.
    chapter_content_id: The UUID of the text content, used for optimistic concurrency control when updating text.

ChapterContentMeta.title: ChapterContentMeta
ChapterContentMeta.description:
Metadata for a ChapterContent.

Attributes:
    chapter_content_version: The version number of the text content, used for optimistic concurrency control when updating text.
    chapter_content_id: The UUID of the text content, used for optimistic concurrency control when updating text.

ChapterData.title: ChapterData
ChapterData.description:
Pydantic schema for aggregating a ChapterContent and a Chapter together.

Attributes:
    metadata: The metadata of the chapter, such as title and whether it's primary.
    content: The text content of the chapter.

CluenerParams.title: CluenerParams
CluenerParams.description:
Pydantic schema for a Cluener model.

Attributes:
    chunk_size: Integer between 1 and 512. Determines max size of chunks passed to NER model. Has default value 500.
    separators: Dictionary of the form `char : SepPriority`. The predict algorithm will prioritize chunks ending in higher priority separators. Has default value (read the code to see what it is.)
    force_chunk: If no separators found in some interval, force the chunker to chunk mid-sentence. Has default value False.

Notes:
    To validate this model without injecting default values, call `CluenerModelParams.model_validate(..., context={'skip_default_values' : True})`.

CreateAutoLabels.title: CreateAutoLabels
CreateAutoLabels.description:
Pydantic schema for creating a new autolabel run.

Attributes:
    novel_id: UUID of novel to create auto labels for.
    params: Parameters for the NER model. Discriminated by model_name attribute.
    chapter_ids: Optional. Restrict to specific chapter UUIDs.
    start: Optional. Restrict to chapters with number >= start.
    end: Optional. Restrict to chapters with number < end.
    is_public: Optional. Restrict to chapters with this specific public flag.

CreateAutoLabelsResponse.title: CreateAutoLabelsResponse
CreateAutoLabelsResponse.description:
Pydantic schema for the response after creating autolabels.

Attributes:
    run: The autolabel run that was created.
    autolabels: The list of autolabel entries created in this run.

CreateChapter.title: CreateChapter
CreateChapter.description:
Pydantic schema to validate data for creating a new chapter. The novel_id is expected to be passed via the URL path.

Attributes:
    chapter_num: The chapter number to create.
    chapter_title: Title of the chapter. Defaults to empty string.
    chapter_is_public: Whether the chapter is publicly visible. Defaults to False.

CreateLabelData.title: CreateLabelData
CreateLabelData.description:
Pydantic schema for validating create requests for label data.

Attributes:
    chapter_content_id: UUID of chapter content being labelled.

CreateLabelDataByAutoLabel.title: CreateLabelDataByAutoLabel
CreateLabelDataByAutoLabel.description:
Pydantic schema to specifiy a set of AutoLabels to be moved to LabelDatas.

Attributes:
    run_id: UUID of the autolabel run whose results should be promoted.
    chapter_ids: Optional filter on what chapters to include.
    start: Optional filter on the least chapter number to include.
    end: Optional filter on the greatest chapter number to include.

CreateLabelDataByAutoLabelStatus.title: CreateLabelDataByAutoLabelStatus
CreateLabelDataByAutoLabelStatus.description:
Return message for CreateLabelDataByAutoLabel.

Attributes:
    success: List of tuples of (chapter_id, chapter_content_id) for successful inserts.
    errors: List of tuples of (chapter_id, chapter_content_id, error message) for failed inserts.

CreateLabelGroup.title: CreateLabelGroup
CreateLabelGroup.description:
Pydantic schema for validating forms for creating a label group.

Attributes:
    label_group_name: Name of label group to create.
    novel_id: UUID of novel this label group belongs to.

CreateNovel.title: CreateNovel
CreateNovel.description:
Pydantic schema to validate forms for creating a novel.

Attributes:
    novel_title: Novel title to create.
    novel_description: Description of novel we are creating.
    novel_author: Author of novel we are creating.
    novel_visibility: Visibility level of novel we are creating.
    novel_type: Type of novel we are creating.
    language_code: String code key to language of novel we are creating.
    source_work_id: Optional source work to attach to. If None, a new source work is auto-created.

CreateSourceWork.title: CreateSourceWork
CreateSourceWork.description:
Pydantic schema to validate forms for creating a source work.

Attributes:
    source_work_title: Title of the source work to create.
    source_work_description: Optional description.

CreateUser.title: CreateUser
CreateUser.description:
Pydantic schema to validate data required to create a user.

Attributes:
    user_name: Username of user being created.
    user_password: Unhashed password of user being create.
    user_type: UserType type of user to be created.
Notes:

DeleteLabelOp.title: DeleteLabelOp
DeleteLabelOp.description:
Pydantic schema for a label delete operation. Inherits all attributes from LabelOpBase.

Attributes:
    op: The string literal 'delete'.

DeleteUserStatus.title: DeleteUserStatus
DeleteUserStatus.description:
Pydantic schema to return status after deleting a user

Attributes:
    status: One of 'success', 'fail', 'verify'.
    detail: String denoting details of operation.

DetailHTTPErrorResponse.title: DetailHTTPErrorResponse
DetailHTTPErrorResponse.description:
Generic error payload for HTTPException responses that only return a detail string.

Attributes:
    detail: Human-readable description of the error.

DoNothingParams.title: DoNothingParams
DoNothingParams.description:
Pydantic schema for a DoNothing model.

Attributes:
    model_name: Literal "do_nothing".

EditChapterData.title: EditChapterData
EditChapterData.description:
Pydantic schema for the data needed to edit a chapter.

Attributes:
    chapter_content: ChapterContent being edited.
    no_label_data: List of LabelGroup objects that have no label data associated with them.

Label.title: Label
Label.description:
Pydantic schema for a label that belongs to a LabelData.
Extends LabelBase with a label_data_id foreign key and label_id pkey.

LabelBase.title: LabelBase
LabelBase.description:
Pydantic schema for a single label without a parent LabelData reference.
Used by autolabels and other contexts where labels exist independently of a LabelData.

Attributes:
    label_entity_group: Some arbitrary string denoting the entity group this label belongs to (e.g. PERSON, LOCATION, etc.).
    label_score: Some float corresponding to how likely a label is to be correct. Used by label autogeneration modules (TBD).
    label_word: Word that this label is labeling.
    label_start: Start position of this label in text.
    label_end: End position of this label in text.
    label_dirty: Use TBD, most likely will be for score calculations in LabelData aggregate operations.

Note:
    label_start must be strictly less than label_end. Otherwise a ValueError will occur.
    label_word must have length label_end - label_start. Otherwise a ValueError will occur.

LabelContributor.title: LabelContributor
LabelContributor.description:
Pydantic schema for a label contributor, which can be either a user or an autolabel model.

Attributes:
    label_contributor_role: Role of the contributor, either a user or an autolabel model.
    label_group_id: UUID of label group this contributor belongs to.
    user_id: UUID of user

LabelData.title: LabelData
LabelData.description:
Pydantic schema for a list of labels in some text.

Attributes:
    label_data_id: UUID identifier for this LabelData.
    label_group_id: UUID of label group this LabelData belongs to.
    chapter_content_id: UUID of chapter content this LabelData is labelling.

LabelGroup.title: LabelGroup
LabelGroup.description:
Pydantic schema for a label group.

Attributes:
    label_group_id: UUID identifier for this label group.
    label_group_name: Name of this label group.
    novel_id: UUID of novel this label group belongs to.

LabelGroupWithRole.title: LabelGroupWithRole
LabelGroupWithRole.description:
Pydantic schema for a label group with a user's role in that label group.

Attributes:
    label_group: LabelGroup object.
    role: Role of the user in this label group.

Language.title: Language
Language.description:
Pydantic schema for language.

Attributes:
    language_code: String code key to language.
    language_name: String name of language.

ModifyChapterContentResponse.title: ModifyChapterContentResponse
ModifyChapterContentResponse.description:
Pydantic schema for the response after modifying chapter content.

Attributes:
    chapter_content_version: The new version number of the text content after applying the modifications.
    chapter_content_id: The UUID of the text content that was modified.
    label_data_id_map: A mapping from label data IDs before the text modification to label data IDs after the text modification

Novel.title: Novel
Novel.description:
Pydantic schema for novel.

Attributes:
    novel_id: UUID id of novel in db.
    novel_title: String title of novel.
    novel_description: String summary or description of novel.
    novel_author: String author or description of novel.
    novel_visibility: Visibility enum of novel.
    novel_type: NovelType enum of novel.
    language_code: String code key to language of the novel.
    source_work_id: UUID foreign key to source work of the novel.

NovelAndUsers.title: NovelAndUsers
NovelAndUsers.description:
Pydantic schema for novel and its associated users.

Attributes:
    novel: The novel metadata.
    users: A list of users associated with this novel.

NovelContributor.title: NovelContributor
NovelContributor.description:
Pydantic schema for a novel contributor.

Attributes:
    user_id: UUID of the user.
    novel_id: UUID of the novel.
    contributor_role: Role of the user in the novel.

OperationStatus.title: OperationStatus
OperationStatus.description:
Pydantic model to signal return status of operation.

Attributes:
    status: One of "success", "fail".
    detail: Details on operation.

Notes:
    Unless under exceptional circumstances, should not return fail and just raise an exception.

RequestConflictDetail.title: RequestConflictDetail
RequestConflictDetail.description:
Structured detail payload used by request-key wrapped 409 responses.

Attributes:
    detail: Human-readable description of the error.
    cache_conflict: Whether the failure was caused by a request-key cache conflict.

RequestConflictErrorResponse.title: RequestConflictErrorResponse
RequestConflictErrorResponse.description:
HTTPException response body for request-key wrapped 409 responses.

Attributes:
    detail: Structured conflict detail.

SourceWork.title: SourceWork
SourceWork.description:
Pydantic schema for a source work.

Attributes:
    source_work_id: UUID id of source work in db.
    source_work_title: Title of the source work.
    source_work_description: Optional description of the source work.

SourceWorkData-Input.title: SourceWorkData
SourceWorkData-Input.description:
Pydantic schema to represent a source work and all its associated novels.

Attributes:
    source_work: The source work metadata.
    novels: A list of novels associated with this source work.

SourceWorkData-Output.title: SourceWorkData
SourceWorkData-Output.description:
Pydantic schema to represent a source work and all its associated novels.

Attributes:
    source_work: The source work metadata.
    novels: A list of novels associated with this source work.

TextOp.title: TextOp
TextOp.description:
Pydantic schema to update text content of a chapter.

Attributes:
    op: The text operation, either "insert" or "delete".
    start: The starting index in the text content where the operation should be applied.
    text: The text to insert (for insert operations) or the text to delete (for delete operations).

Token.title: Token
Token.description:
Pydantic schema for a JWT

Attributes:
    access_token: Payload.
    token_type: Type of token.

UpdateChapter.title: UpdateChapter
UpdateChapter.description:
Pydantic schema to validate data for updating chapter metadata.

Attributes:
    chapter_title: The new title for the chapter.

UpdateChapterContent.title: UpdateChapterContent
UpdateChapterContent.description:
Pydantic schema to validate data for updating the text content of a chapter. The chapter_id is expected to be passed via the URL path.

Attributes:
    text_ops: A list of text operations (insertions or deletions) to apply to the existing text content.
    chapter_content_id: The UUID of the text content, used for optimistic concurrency control when updating text.

UpdateLabelDataStream.title: UpdateLabelDataStream
UpdateLabelDataStream.description:
Pydantic schema for a buffered stream of label operations.

Attributes:
    ops: A list of label operations.

UpdateLabelGroup.title: UpdateLabelGroup
UpdateLabelGroup.description:
Pydantic schema for validating forms for updating a label group.

Attributes:
    label_group_name: New name of label group.

UpdateLabelOp.title: UpdateLabelOp
UpdateLabelOp.description:
Pydantic schema for a label update operation. Inherits all attributes from LabelOpBase.

Attributes:
    op: The string literal 'update'.
    new_start_pos: Optional parameter. The new start position of the label.
    new_end_pos: Optional parameter. The new end position of the label.
    new_word: Optional parameter. The new word the label is labelling. Must satisfy `new_word == chapter_text[new_start_pos : new_end_pos]`.
    dirty: Optional parameter. Value to change the current label's dirty value to.
    entity_group: Optional parameter. New entity group for this label.
    score: Optional parameter. New score for the entity.

UpdateNovel.title: UpdateNovel
UpdateNovel.description:
Pydantic schema to validate forms for updating a novel. The novel id will be passed into the router endpoint.

Attributes:
    novel_title: Updated title to novel we are updating. If None, then do not update.
    novel_description: Updated description of novel we are updating. If None, then do not update.
    novel_author: Author of novel we are creating. If None, then do not update.
    novel_visibility: Updated visibility level of novel we are updating. If None, then do not update.
    novel_type: Updated novel type. If None, then do not update.

UpdateSourceWork.title: UpdateSourceWork
UpdateSourceWork.description:
Pydantic schema to validate forms for updating a source work.

Attributes:
    source_work_title: Updated title. If None, do not update.
    source_work_description: Updated description. If None, do not update.

User.title: User
User.description:
Pydantic model for user information.

Attributes:
    user_id: id of this user.
    user_name: Username for this user.
    user_type: A value in UserType (e.g. 'admin', or 'user')
